job.name: Daily Big Tech industry research roles (US)

payload.message:
This is a daily job-search + email task for Jonggyu.

Goal: Find current US-based industry research positions at these companies only: Google, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, Apple, NVIDIA, OpenAI.

Candidate profile source:
- Primary: /Users/jonggyu/.openclaw/workspace/resume.pdf
- If direct PDF parsing is inconvenient, use /Users/jonggyu/.openclaw/workspace/resume_profile.txt

What to do each run:
1) Gather fresh postings from official careers pages (or reliable direct job listing pages) for the listed companies.
2) Filter for roles that fit this profile: systems/storage/kernel/virtualization/datacenter/power-control research/engineering research.
3) Keep US-only roles.
4) Produce a concise summary with up to 12 best matches:
   - Role title
   - Company
   - Location
   - Why it matches (1 line)
   - URL
5) Email the summary to jonggyu.pk@gmail.com using gog CLI via exec:
   - subject: "Daily Big Tech Research Roles - <YYYY-MM-DD>"
   - plain text body
   - command pattern: gog gmail send --to jonggyu.pk@gmail.com --subject "..." --body-file - <<'EOF' ... EOF

If there are no good matches, email a short "no strong matches today" note.

If gog auth is missing, announce that clearly in the run summary and include exact setup commands:
- gog auth credentials /path/to/client_secret.json
- gog auth add <your-email> --services gmail
- gog auth list

tool_params.content:
Jonggyu Park — Resume profile for role matching

Target role family:
- Industry research roles in systems and infrastructure.
- Strong fit: Research Scientist, Research Engineer, Applied Scientist (systems), Performance/Systems Engineer with research focus.

Core strengths:
- Storage systems, Linux kernel resource management, virtualization, datacenter power control.
- Systems programming in C, Python, Go, eBPF.
- Filesystem internals (ext4, F2FS, Btrfs), SSD/NVMe, SPDK, block I/O, caching/QoS.
- KVM/QEMU live migration, containers/Kubernetes, CXL memory pooling.

Publication/patent signal:
- First-author publications at SOSP, EuroSys, PPoPP.
- 7 US patents.

Recent experience highlights:
- UW Postdoc (2023–present): prediction-informed server power control, PASS power-adaptive storage server, CXL-based live VM migration, power-aware cloud control plane.
- Prior SKKU work: schedulable lock primitives; defragmentation and storage performance systems; kernel QoS scheduling.

Location filter for this pipeline:
- US-only postings.

Company focus for this pipeline:
- Google, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, Apple, NVIDIA, OpenAI.

tool_params.command: git -C /Users/jonggyu/.openclaw/workspace add resume_profile.txt && git -C /Users/jonggyu/.openclaw/workspace commit -m "Add resume profile summary for daily role-matching automation"

tool_params.content:
# Daily Big Tech Research Roles — 2026-03-24

Generated: 2026-03-24 10:15 PT
Scope: US-only roles at Google, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, Apple, NVIDIA, OpenAI

## Run Status
- Test run triggered manually from chat.
- Current environment has intermittent gateway timeout for direct cron `run` calls.
- Web search API key is not configured in this environment, so results below are based on the latest successful collection from today.
- Microsoft/OpenAI/Apple listings were not reliably queryable in the earlier run due site/API access constraints.

## Top Matches (US, resume-aligned)

1. **Staff Software Engineer, Storage**  
   **Company:** Google  
   **Location:** Sunnyvale, CA, USA  
   **Why it matches:** Direct storage + kernel/low-level systems focus (C, kernel, large-scale infrastructure).  
   **URL:** https://www.google.com/about/careers/applications/jobs/results/96730118986048198-staff-software-engineer-storage?q=systems%20storage%20kernel&location=United%20States

2. **Software Engineering Manager, Container Optimized Operating System**  
   **Company:** Google  
   **Location:** Sunnyvale, CA, USA  
   **Why it matches:** Linux/container OS + distributed infrastructure; strong systems/platform alignment.  
   **URL:** https://www.google.com/about/careers/applications/jobs/results/85544757131911878-software-engineering-manager-container-optimized-operating-system?q=systems%20storage%20kernel&location=United%20States

3. **Senior Software Engineer, Managed Lustre Dataplane**  
   **Company:** Google  
   **Location:** Kirkland/Seattle, WA, USA  
   **Why it matches:** Storage dataplane role (distributed storage/systems programming in C).  
   **URL:** https://www.google.com/about/careers/applications/jobs/results/80082580933616326-senior-software-engineer-managed-lustre-dataplane?q=systems%20storage%20kernel&location=United%20States

4. **Staff Software Engineer, Accelerator Platform Software**  
   **Company:** Google  
   **Location:** Sunnyvale, CA, USA  
   **Why it matches:** Linux kernel + platform systems software; close to low-level infra research/engineering.  
   **URL:** https://www.google.com/about/careers/applications/jobs/results/115816746417300166-staff-software-engineer-accelerator-platform-software

---
If no strong new matches are found in future runs, this report will still be created with a brief "No strong matches today" note.

# 2026-03-24

- User asked for daily tracking of US industry research positions matching their resume, focused on big tech.
- User preferences for this automation:
  - Schedule: every day at 8:00 AM PT
  - Region: US only
  - Companies: Google, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, Apple, NVIDIA, OpenAI
- Resume location provided by user: `/Users/jonggyu/.openclaw/workspace/resume.pdf`.
- Created helper profile file for matching quality: `/Users/jonggyu/.openclaw/workspace/resume_profile.txt`.
- Cron job created and updated:
  - Name: "Daily Big Tech industry research roles (US)"
  - Job ID: `047ecef4-46a5-4fe2-a07f-1e44d82b357e`
  - Delivery switched from email to local Desktop file output per user request.
- User explicitly changed output preference:
  - Do NOT email results.
  - Save daily report to: `/Users/jonggyu/Desktop/daily-big-tech-research-roles-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md`
- A test output file was generated at:
  - `/Users/jonggyu/Desktop/daily-big-tech-research-roles-2026-03-24.md`
- Operational notes:
  - `gog` Gmail auth is currently not configured (no tokens), but no longer needed for this workflow.
  - Direct cron run attempts from tool showed intermittent gateway timeout; scheduled daily run remains configured.

tool_params.command: gog auth list || true && gog drive search "1042-S 2024" --max 20 2>&1 | head -n 80

python3 - <<'PY'
import os, subprocess, re, json, sys
roots = [
    '/Users/jonggyu/Desktop',
    '/Users/jonggyu/Documents',
    '/Users/jonggyu/Downloads',
    '/Users/jonggyu/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs',
    '/Users/jonggyu/Library/CloudStorage',
]
max_files = 2500
pdfs = []
for root in roots:
    if not os.path.exists(root):
        continue
    for dp, dn, fn in os.walk(root):
        # skip obvious heavy/system dirs
        low = dp.lower()
        if any(x in low for x in ['/library/caches', '/node_modules/', '/.git/']):
            continue
        for f in fn:
            if f.lower().endswith('.pdf'):
                pdfs.append(os.path.join(dp, f))
                if len(pdfs) >= max_files:
                    break
        if len(pdfs) >= max_files:
            break
    if len(pdfs) >= max_files:
        break

hits = []
pat_main = re.compile(r'\b1042\s*[- ]?s\b', re.I)
pat_2024 = re.compile(r'\b2024\b')
for i,p in enumerate(pdfs,1):
    try:
        # first try first 2 pages (faster)
        cmd = ['pdftotext', '-f', '1', '-l', '2', p, '-']
        r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=8)
        txt = (r.stdout or '') + '\n' + (r.stderr or '')
        m1 = bool(pat_main.search(txt))
        m2 = bool(pat_2024.search(txt))
        # If 1042-S found but not 2024, scan more pages up to 10 for year
        if m1 and not m2:
            r2 = subprocess.run(['pdftotext', '-f', '1', '-l', '10', p, '-'], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15)
            txt2 = (r2.stdout or '') + '\n' + (r2.stderr or '')
            m2 = bool(pat_2024.search(txt2))
            txt = txt2
        if m1:
            # rough context line sample
            lines = [ln.strip() for ln in txt.splitlines() if re.search(r'1042\s*[- ]?s|2024|2025|form\s+1042', ln, re.I)]
            sample = '; '.join(lines[:6])
            hits.append({'path': p, 'has2024': m2, 'sample': sample[:500]})
    except Exception:
        pass

print(json.dumps({'scanned_pdfs': len(pdfs), 'hits': hits[:120]}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
PY
